rationale_for_baseline_tier: A 5-day-old neonate with rapidly progressive depressed consciousness, abnormal posturing/bicycling movements, fast deep (acidotic) breathing and a pathognomonic sweet odour is in a life-threatening metabolic emergency with impending seizures and cerebral oedema. This is an immediate threat to life — T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier. Definitive care (IV glucose, toxin removal, PICU) cannot be delivered via telehealth; the family-deliverable levers are ceasing all protein feeds, airway-safe positioning and warmth while awaiting paramedics.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Neonate barely rousable / not feeding with fast heavy breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately — within minutes, not hours
escalation_edges.rationale: A neonate with depressed consciousness and respiratory compensation for acidosis can arrest; emergency ambulance and hospital care are required now.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Your baby is seriously unwell and needs an ambulance right now — please call 000 and tell them a 5-day-old is barely responding and breathing fast. Stay on the line with me and with them.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Seizure activity, apnoea (pauses in breathing) or colour change / collapse
escalation_edges.rationale: Seizures or apnoea signal critical deterioration; lay the baby on his side, keep the airway clear and let paramedics manage — begin resuscitation only if he stops breathing and 000 guides you.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he starts jerking, stops breathing, or changes colour, keep him on his side, keep his airway clear, and tell 000 straight away — they will talk you through what to do.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Any temptation or advice to keep feeding the baby
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stop feeds now and keep them stopped until the emergency team advises
escalation_edges.rationale: Continued protein feeding is directly neurotoxic in this crisis; ceasing all feeds is a mandatory, family-deliverable safety action alongside escalation.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Please stop all feeds now — no milk of any kind and nothing by mouth — because feeding is making him worse. The hospital will manage his nutrition.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: 5-day-old neonate — non-verbal, fully dependent, and physiologically fragile
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: A neonate cannot self-report and deteriorates quickly; the threshold to call 000 is minimal and any home dosing is prohibited (no paediatric dose). All objective assessment and treatment happen with paramedics and in hospital.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: First-time parents managing a terrifying emergency alone at home
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Give clear, single-step instructions (call 000, stop feeds, position on the side, stay with him, unlock the door) and stay with them on the line to reduce panic and delay.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and say a 5-day-old baby is barely responding and breathing fast. Stop all feeds immediately — no formula and no breast milk, and nothing by mouth. Lay him on his side with his head straight to keep his airway clear during the stiff movements, keep him warm, and stay with him. Unlock the front door for the paramedics and stay on the line.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 is available 24 hours a day — this is an emergency and must not wait for clinic hours or a call-back.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The hospital will provide the diagnosis and an emergency metabolic management plan; the GP and the metabolic/genetics service will coordinate lifelong specialist dietary care and genetic counselling.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a deteriorating, barely-responsive neonate as a routine or urgent-but-not-emergency feeding problem instead of calling 000.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Ongoing neurotoxicity, seizures, cerebral oedema and death from delayed emergency care and continued protein feeding.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising self-transport to an emergency department rather than an emergency ambulance for a neonate who may seize or arrest en route without paramedic airway and resuscitation support.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Over-triage is not a meaningful risk here — the presentation is a genuine T5 emergency.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The exact diagnosis (MSUD versus another inborn error or sepsis) cannot be established via telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Act on the emergency, not the label: escalate to 000, cease all protein feeds, and let the hospital establish the diagnosis — the safety-critical actions are identical across the plausible causes.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Delaying escalation to pin down the diagnosis, attempting home tests or glucose, or reassuring the family that a sleepy poorly-feeding neonate is normal.
